text: Joanne, praying for Emily today as she starts her clinical rotations. Big week for her!
Oh Reverend, thank you. She's so nervous she barely ate breakfast, and I told her nerves just mean she cares.
That's the mark of a good nurse if I ever heard one. You'd know better than most.
Forty-one years on the floor taught me that much. Wish I could still show her the ropes myself instead of just praying from my porch.
Your prayers carry plenty of weight, I promise you that. Maybe more than the ropes would.
Ha! Well don't tell Dot Henderson that, she thinks my morning walks are what really keep this town running.
Two miles a day and the Lord's ear — Portland's in good hands with you two.
You're a mess, Reverend. See you Sunday, I'll bring Karen's lemon bars if Emily doesn't eat them all first.
Walter, quick one - Emily's got clinicals this week so I'll be late to fold the bulletins Thursday, that alright?
Course it's alright, Joanne. Dot can help me, she's usually there anyway
Bless you. Dot and I did our walk this morning and she near talked my ear off about the new choir director lol
Ha, she's been talking his ear off too. You still coming Sunday for the potluck?
Wouldn't miss it. Bringing my elderberry syrup, half the pew's got the sniffles going around
Good, I'll skip the cough drops then 😂 how's Brian doing, haven't seen him in a while
He's fine, just busy with work, says hi though. Alright, off to watch my shows, tell Thursday's fine!
Enjoy your programs, Joanne. See you Sunday
Morning Joanne! Your dried elderberries and nettle leaf came in early this week, 10 lbs each like you asked
Oh good, Nancy, that's a relief! My little herb group meets Thursday and we were nearly bare on nettle
Ha, I figured. Also got a fresh batch of chamomile flowers if you want to add some, smells wonderful
Go ahead and throw in two pounds, I'll come by the shop Wednesday after my walk with Dot
Sounds good. Same invoice as last time, or should I bill it separate for the community stock?
Separate please, that batch is for the clinic not my own tea cabinet
Will do. Oh, and tell Emily good luck, my niece did her nursing clinicals at the same hospital
I will, she's got one this week actually. Thank you, Nancy, see you Wednesday
Joanne! Heard through the grapevine your granddaughter's doing her clinicals at St. Vincent's. That where you started out?
Emily, yes! Started her rotations this week, nervous as a cat. I told her St. Vincent's will either make her or break her, same as it did me.
Ha! Made me, mostly. Tell her the night shift charge nurses are the ones to befriend, not the doctors.
That is exactly what I told her, George. Some things never change in forty years, praise God.
How's retirement treating you? Still doing the herb thing?
Growing more than I can use. Made Dot Henderson a tincture for her knees last week and she near cried, said it worked better than the pharmacy stuff.
Dot Henderson, your walking partner? Small world, my wife knows her from church.
It surely is small. Tell your wife I said hello, and tell Emily to call her old granny if St. Vincent's gets rough.
